description: Seed patterns for the audit/config-deployment family. Hits target container / IaC defaults that ship insecure settings: root containers, debug flags in production, secrets in source, exposed metadata endpoints, permissive CORS / TLS defaults.
notes: Most hits will be in YAML / Dockerfile / HCL. The skill should read the whole file, not just the matched line.
Many IaC concerns require knowing the deployment context (which env this applies to). The skill should be conservative when context is ambiguous.

dockerfile.false_positive_notes: Presence is positive; absence is the concern (default root).

dockerfile.description: RUN that pipes curl/wget to shell
dockerfile.false_positive_notes: Common but worth flagging — no checksum / signature verification.
Prefer COPY + explicit download with checksum.
Informational; verify the port matters and is intended.

k8s_manifests.description: Pod uses host network / PID / IPC
k8s_manifests.false_positive_notes: Worth flagging unless explicitly justified (e.g. CNI, monitoring).
Investigate each granted cap; SYS_ADMIN ≈ root.

k8s_manifests.description: Env var sourced from a Secret (or naming hint)
k8s_manifests.false_positive_notes: Common; concern is plaintext secrets, not Secret references.

terraform.description: Terraform IAM with Action=* or Resource=*

terraform.false_positive_notes: Sometimes intentional (CDN, public HTTP); check the port.

env_config.false_positive_notes: Concern only if in production config; check filename / branch.
May be a placeholder; check the value (`changeme`, `password`, `test` are placeholders).

php_config.description: wp-config.php WP_DEBUG true — leaks errors + paths
php_config.false_positive_notes: OK in local dev; always wrong in prod.
wp-config-sample.php has these; wp-config.php should not.
WordPress requires these in config; ensure file is not in git.

php_config.description: Laravel .env APP_DEBUG=true / APP_ENV=local — exposes stack traces
php_config.false_positive_notes: Concern only in .env files committed to prod branches.

php_config.description: php.ini display_errors=On — leaks errors to clients
php_config.false_positive_notes: OK in dev; never in prod.

csharp_config.description: appsettings.json detailed errors / debug logging in prod
csharp_config.false_positive_notes: OK in appsettings.Development.json; concern in appsettings.json or appsettings.Production.json.

csharp_config.description: appsettings.json connection string with embedded password
csharp_config.false_positive_notes: Should use User Secrets / Azure Key Vault / env vars.

csharp_config.description: web.config debug=true / customErrors=Off — leaks stack traces
.csproj PackageReference for libs with historical CVEs — check version
csharp_config.false_positive_notes: Always present; check specific version against CVE database.
Concern when in main branch / release builds.

java_config.description: Spring application.yml debug / SQL logging enabled — leaks query info in prod
java_config.false_positive_notes: OK in application-dev.yml; concern in application.yml or application-prod.yml.

java_config.description: Spring Actuator endpoints exposed — env, heapdump, metrics leaks
java_config.false_positive_notes: include=* exposes everything; check for auth in same config.

java_config.description: Spring H2 console enabled — DB UI exposure
CSRF disabled in Spring Security config
DB password in application properties — should be env var / secret manager
java_config.false_positive_notes: `${DB_PASSWORD}` template ref is OK; literal value is the concern.

java_config.description: pom.xml artifactId with historical CVE pattern — investigate <version>
java_config.false_positive_notes: Almost always present; check version against known CVEs (log4j 2.x<2.17.1 = log4shell).

java_config.description: web.xml security-constraint definitions — surface for access-control review
java_config.false_positive_notes: Presence is good; absence on a deployed app is the concern.

java_config.description: log4j2 JNDI lookup references — Log4Shell pattern
java_config.false_positive_notes: Critical; log4j 2.x<2.17.1 enables this by default.

java_config.description: Hibernate SQL logging enabled — leaks queries + values in prod
java_config.false_positive_notes: OK in dev; concern in prod.

java_config.description: Tomcat context.xml privileged / allowLinking — escapes the webapp sandbox
